claim: A 2022 study estimated agricultural residue burning contributes 20-30 percent of peak-season PM2.5 in Delhi and 10-15 percent across the Indo-Gangetic Plain during post-harvest periods.

source.title: Agricultural residue burning and air quality in South Asia: a review

method: Reviewed source-apportionment studies, fire-detection data, and health-impact estimates.
limitations: Fire-detection by MODIS misses small fires.
Source-attribution is model-dependent and has uncertainty.
